decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on deploying aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also utilizes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory to boost the team's power and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck includes utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland to provide flexibility in dealing with opposing threats. Overall, the goal is to maintain pressure on the opponent and secure victory through a combination of efficient creatures and powerful support spells.

This deck focuses on disrupting your opponent's game plan while putting pressure on them with efficient creatures and spells. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Fatal Push to disrupt their strategy, while deploying threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, Murktide Regent, and Orcish Bowmasters to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes powerful tempo plays like Daze and Brazen Borrower to further disrupt the opponent and keep them on the back foot. This deck aims to control the game early and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to recover.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up artifact creatures onto the battlefield and disrupting opponents' strategies with cards like Chalice of the Void and Force of Will. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recurring artifacts from the graveyard, while Urza's Saga provides additional artifact support. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of aggro strategies and synergistic artifact cards, while also maintaining control through disruption and card advantage. Lavaspur Boots and Shadowspear provide additional versatility and protection for key creatures. Overall, the deck is designed to quickly build a board presence and apply pressure while disrupting opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through disruption and removal spells while pressuring the opponent with threats that are difficult to deal with. By combining efficient interactive cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will with powerful creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while presenting a fast clock. The inclusion of cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Brazen Borrower provides additional versatility and card advantage to stay ahead in the game. The deck also utilizes land disruption with Wasteland to further disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of creatures, spells, and removal cards to maintain board control and pressure the opponent. It utilizes a combination of powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls, along with efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The inclusion of cards like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison adds versatility and disruption to the strategy. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the aggressive game plan, with cards like Flooded Strand and Plateau providing color fixing and utility. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently.

This deck focuses on deploying aggressive threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. By combining early pressure with disruption, it aims to control the tempo of the game and prevent the opponent from stabilizing. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Daze, Fatal Push, and Force of Will, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's resources and protect its own threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide card advantage and powerful threats to close out the game quickly. Overall, the deck seeks to be proactive and efficient in its gameplay.

This deck focuses on controlling the pace of the game through disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, while utilizing efficient creatures like Tamiyo and Brazen Borrower to pressure the opponent's life total. The inclusion of aggressive cards like Orcish Bowmasters and Moonshadow allows the deck to apply early pressure and maintain tempo advantage. Wasteland disrupts the opponent's mana base, while Daze and Snuff Out provide answers to threats that slip through the cracks. The deck aims to outpace the opponent and maintain control of the battlefield to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo, using cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Thassa's Oracle to win the game. It includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo, as well as cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for important pieces. The deck also includes mana acceleration in the form of Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, and land disruption with cards like Wasteland. Overall, the deck aims to set up and execute the Doomsday combo efficiently while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and counter spells like Force of Will, while also applying pressure with efficient creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. It aims to control the tempo of the game by making optimal use of spells like Daze and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's resources and prevent them from stabilizing. Additionally, the inclusion of planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and additional disruption options to maintain the deck's aggressive tempo.

The main focus of this deck is utilizing artifacts to generate mana and card advantage in order to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By using cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Bauble to ramp up mana production, as well as powerful artifact creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Patchwork Automaton, the deck aims to apply early pressure and control the board in order to secure a quick victory. Additionally, cards like Force of Will provide disruption to opponent's strategies, while cards like Urza's Saga and Pithing Needle help to further control the game state.

The deck aims to quickly and efficiently combo off by milling the entire library, using cards like Undercity Informer and Balustrade Spy. Thassa's Oracle is used as a win condition to win the game on the spot after milling out the opponent's deck. The deck utilizes a lot of fast mana and disruptive spells to protect the combo, with Pact of Negation to counter any potential disruption. The deck aims to win quickly and decisively, without relying on traditional creature combat to secure victory.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using Cloudpost and Urza's lands, while also utilizing Eldrazi creatures like Ulamog and Emrakul in combination with powerful cards like Karn and Ugin to control the game and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Crop Rotation and Once Upon a Time to search for key pieces and ensure consistency in the combo strategy. The goal is to set up a dominant board state and win the game through a combination of ramping, controlling, and high-powered creatures.

The key concept of this Mardu Aggro deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while aggressively putting pressure on them with efficient creatures and removal spells. The deck utilizes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's hand while using powerful aggro creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls to quickly close out the game. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and ways to deal damage, making it a versatile and powerful aggressive deck in the Legacy format.

The deck focuses on aggressive gameplay with a combination of disruptive creatures and spells. By utilizing the Aether Vial, the deck can quickly deploy creatures and disrupt the opponent's strategy with countermagic like Force of Will and disruptive creatures like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade. The deck also includes a variety of creatures with unique abilities that can help control the board and apply pressure on the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggression and disruption to secure victory.

This deck is focused on utilizing fast mana acceleration to quickly generate storm count and cast a lethal Tendrils of Agony or Ad Nauseam to win the game. It includes various tutors and disruption spells to help find and protect the combo pieces, as well as some utility cards like Wishclaw Talisman and Urza's Bauble. The deck's synergy and consistency allow it to effectively execute its combo strategy and overwhelm the opponent with a high storm count finish. The addition of cards like Beseech the Mirror and Gaea's Will provide additional versatility and options for winning the game.

The main concept of this Aluren combo deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the pieces needed to play powerful creatures for free using cards like Show and Tell and Aluren. By utilizing cards such as Force of Will and Veil of Summer for protection, the deck aims to win the game by casting big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier for free with the help of Omniscience. This deck focuses on cheating big creatures into play and overwhelming the opponent before they have a chance to respond.
